title: Vietnam's President To Lam Makes First State Visit to India Under Modi
lede: Vietnam's newly inaugurated President To Lam arrived in India on May 5, 2026, for his first State Visit, signaling deepening diplomatic engagement between New Delhi and Hanoi at a critical moment in Southeast Asian geopolitics.

sections.context: President To Lam assumed the presidency of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam on April 7, 2026, and embarked on his inaugural State Visit to India just days later at the invitation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. This timing is significant: the visit occurs within a month of his assumption of office, underscoring India's strategic priority in Vietnam's early diplomatic calendar. The visit began in Bodh Gaya with visits to the Mahabodhi Temple and the Vietnam Temple—culturally resonant sites that reflect both civilizational connections and Vietnam's Buddhist heritage. President To Lam had previously visited India in April 2023 in his capacity as Minister of Public Security, indicating a trajectory of growing engagement. The visit runs from May 5-7, 2026, and includes a high-level ministerial and official delegation alongside a substantial business delegation, suggesting multi-dimensional engagement beyond state-to-state relations.
sections.threads: This State Visit represents the second in-person meeting between Prime Minister Modi and President To Lam, the first having occurred in September 2024 at the UNGA Summit of the Future in New York. The ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan, combined with President To Lam's visits to both Rajghat and Ho Chi Minh's statue, demonstrates the symbolic importance both nations attach to historical legacies—Gandhian non-violence and Ho Chi Minh's anti-colonial struggle—in framing their contemporary partnership. The presence of a large business delegation alongside official representatives suggests efforts to broaden bilateral ties beyond traditional security and diplomatic channels into economic and commercial domains, consistent with India's broader Southeast Asia engagement strategy.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor the outcomes of the 'wide-ranging and substantive discussions' held between Modi and To Lam at Hyderabad House, as these will likely signal priority areas for the India-Vietnam relationship in the coming year. The composition of To Lam's delegation—spanning ministerial and business representatives—indicates readiness for sectoral collaboration that may extend to defense, technology, and trade partnerships. The timing of this visit, early in To Lam's presidency, will shape how Vietnam calibrates its great power relationships and ASEAN positioning, making it a critical juncture for tracking India's influence in Southeast Asian strategic calculations through 2026-2027.
